entries.mem0ai/mem0: As the official memory provider for Hermes Agent, Mem0 offers a universal memory layer that retains state across users, sessions, and agents. It utilizes a token-efficient, single-pass extraction algorithm and hybrid retrieval to fuse semantic, keyword, and entity-linked signals.
entries.garrytan/gbrain: This opinionated 'agent brain' features a self-wiring knowledge graph that automatically extracts typed entity links from interactions. It enhances Hermes with over 30 MCP tools and a hybrid search system that combines vector retrieval with graph-based ranking.
entries.vectorize-io/hindsight: Hindsight implements a 'learn-by-doing' workflow for agents through dedicated retain, recall, and reflect cycles. It simplifies memory management via a two-line LLM wrapper and ensures privacy through per-user memory isolation using custom metadata filtering.
entries.plastic-labs/honcho: Honcho is a stateful memory library that tracks evolving relationships between users, agents, and groups over time. It provides natural language querying for interaction history and supports both Python and TypeScript SDKs with OpenAI-compatible context formatting.
entries.greyhaven-ai/autocontext: This recursive context harness focuses on task success through iterative improvement, structured traces, and real-world evaluation. It facilitates multi-role cooperation to refine strategies and curate knowledge across various model providers.
entries.stephenschoettler/hermes-lcm: This plugin provides lossless context management for Hermes Agent by utilizing a hierarchical DAG structure for depth-aware compaction. It stores raw conversation history in SQLite and offers specialized tools for grepping and expanding compacted context.
entries.elkimek/honcho-self-hosted: This project enables private, cross-session persistence for Hermes Agent by providing a self-hosted backend for the Honcho library. It includes an automated setup script for Docker and supports any OpenAI-compatible provider or local inference server.
entries.yoloshii/ClawMem: ClawMem is a local-first context engine that operates entirely on-device without cloud dependencies or API keys. It employs a triple-threat hybrid retrieval system—BM25, vector search, and knowledge graphs—integrated via an MCP server.
entries.EfficientContext/ContextPilot: Focused on inference efficiency, ContextPilot optimizes long-context performance through prefix reordering and redundant block deduplication. It natively integrates with Hermes Agent and vLLM to boost cache hits and reduce input token usage.
entries.plur-ai/plur: Plur introduces a shared memory layer using an open YAML 'engram' format for transparent, local storage. It offers zero-cost hybrid search and automates memory injection for Hermes and other coding agents.
entries.keepnotes-ai/keep: Keep provides reflective memory by indexing code, documents, and media with automatic summarization and OCR capabilities. It uses edge tags to build bidirectional relationships between notes, accessible via MCP and CLI interfaces.
entries.AxDSan/mnemosyne: Mnemosyne is a zero-dependency memory system designed for sub-millisecond latency in read, write, and search operations. It relies on local SQLite storage with native vector support, requiring no external databases or API keys.
entries.amanning3390/flowstate-qmd: This anticipatory memory system prefetches context by monitoring agent session logs to stay ahead of the model's needs. Built for the Hermes 2026 Hackathon, it uses local-first storage via SQLite-vec and provides an MCP-native server for coding clients.

entries.mukul975/Anthropic-Cybersecurity-Skills: This repository provides 754 structured cybersecurity skills mapped to major frameworks like MITRE ATT&CK and NIST CSF 2.0. It enables Hermes to perform specialized tasks across 26 domains, including cloud security and malware analysis, using the agentskills.io standard.
entries.AMAP-ML/SkillClaw: SkillClaw introduces an 'Agentic Evolver' that automates skill deduplication and quality improvement through background evolution loops. It unifies skill libraries across multiple devices and sessions, offering native integration for Hermes and Codex.
entries.conorbronsdon/avoid-ai-writing: This tool audits and rewrites content to eliminate 36 distinct AI writing patterns and structural tells. It uses a three-tier word replacement table and a two-pass audit to ensure Hermes produces human-like, non-detectable prose.
entries.wondelai/skills: A cross-platform library that provides Hermes with modular skills for architecture, UX design, and product strategy. It features a dedicated CLI for easy installation and full compatibility with the agentskills.io ecosystem.
entries.DougTrajano/pydantic-ai-skills: This project implements type-safe agentskills.io support specifically for Pydantic AI workflows. It optimizes performance by using progressive disclosure of skill metadata to significantly reduce token consumption.
entries.Agents365-ai/drawio-skill: This skill allows Hermes to generate complex draw.io diagrams from natural language descriptions using specialized presets for machine learning and architecture. It includes automated self-checking loops to refine diagrams and annotate tensor shapes accurately.
entries.Cranot/super-hermes: Super-hermes empowers the agent to write its own analytical prompts and custom thinking instructions for complex problem-solving. It helps Hermes identify structural conservation laws in code and generate reports on its own analysis limitations.
entries.smartcontractkit/chainlink-agent-skills: This library provides standardized skills for interacting with Chainlink's decentralized oracle networks, including CCIP and Data Feeds. It allows Hermes to manage smart contract operations and automated CRE onboarding via the agentskills.io specification.
entries.tlehman/litprog-skill: This literate programming skill enables Hermes to weave runnable code and prose together into .lit.md files. It features a 'tangler' to extract source files and automatically syncs edits between documentation and code.
entries.esaradev/icarus-plugin: Icarus provides Hermes with cross-instance shared memory and a pipeline for training its own replacement models. It extracts decision-quality data from active work to facilitate fine-tuning and long-term memory retention.
entries.chigwell/skilldock.io: Skilldock is a comprehensive registry and SDK for managing the lifecycle of reusable AI skills with recursive dependency resolution. It uniquely integrates commerce rails, allowing for skill transactions using USD or the TON blockchain.
entries.black-forest-labs/skills: This official skill set provides Hermes with precise prompting guidelines and API integration patterns for FLUX image generation models. It supports structured JSON prompting and exact hex color specifications for high-fidelity visual outputs.
entries.Romanescu11/hermes-skill-factory: A meta-skill plugin that monitors active workflows to automatically detect and propose new, reusable skills. It generates the necessary SKILL.md documentation and Python scaffolds to expand Hermes' capabilities on the fly.
entries.ReinaMacCredy/maestro: Maestro is a harness for long-running missions that provides Hermes with structured memory and a plan-approve-execute framework. It facilitates multi-agent collaboration through markdown briefs and a real-time TUI dashboard.
entries.tiann/execplan-skill: This skill enables Hermes to execute complex, multi-step tasks with built-in checkpoints and autonomous recovery procedures. It ensures reliability for long-running processes by providing real-time decision tracking and rollback capabilities.
entries.cablate/Agentic-MCP-Skill: This progressive MCP client uses a three-layer lazy loading architecture to minimize token usage during tool discovery. It maintains persistent server connections via a daemon, allowing Hermes to hot-reload configurations without interruptions.
entries.armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge: Skill Forge converts existing code repositories and developer documentation into version-pinned, agentskills.io-compliant skills. It ensures high reliability by providing verifiable audit trails with file-and-line level provenance.
entries.amanning3390/hermeshub: HermesHub serves as a community marketplace for browsing, searching, and installing skills with one-click simplicity. It prioritizes safety through automated security scanning and an agent-to-agent feedback system for trust scores.
entries.PederHP/skillsdotnet: This project brings the agentskills.io standard to the .NET ecosystem, allowing C# developers to distribute skills as MCP resources. It supports progressive disclosure via YAML frontmatter to keep agent interactions efficient.
entries.adnw-vinc/hermes-nextcloud: This skill integrates Hermes with Nextcloud, enabling the agent to manage files, calendars, and contacts via WebDAV and CalDAV protocols. It includes a guided setup script to ensure secure communication over HTTPS using app passwords.

entries.numtide/llm-agents.nix: This project provides a comprehensive collection of Nix packages for AI coding agents, including Hermes, ensuring they are always up-to-date through automated daily builds. It allows developers to execute agents instantly via Nix flakes, offering a unified and reproducible environment for CLI-based AI tools.
entries.TheAiSingularity/hermesclaw: Hermesclaw focuses on high-security deployments by implementing hardware-level sandboxing through the NVIDIA OpenShell kernel. It offers multi-layer protection that strictly enforces network and filesystem policies, making it the primary choice for running Hermes in sensitive or untrusted environments.
entries.0xrsydn/nix-hermes-agent: This repository provides a dedicated NixOS module for the declarative management of Hermes configurations, documents, and systemd services. It excels at secure secret handling via sops-nix integration, catering to users who require a reproducible, system-level deployment on Nix-based infrastructure.
entries.rookiemann/portable-hermes-agent: Designed for Windows users, this zero-install solution provides a GUI-driven desktop agent equipped with 100 built-in tools and local model support. It eliminates the need for Docker or complex installations, featuring a dynamic tool maker and workflow engine for local automation pipelines.
entries.xmbshwll/hermes-agent-docker: This project offers a streamlined Docker image that bundles the official Hermes installation with mini-swe-agent for a ready-to-use sandbox. It supports persistent state through mounted directories and allows users to target specific Hermes versions using simple build arguments.
entries.jpalmae/hermeshq: HermesHQ serves as a centralized Docker-first control plane for orchestrating multiple agent instances and workspaces from a single web interface. It introduces advanced features like inter-agent delegation, automated task scheduling, and native integrations for Telegram and WhatsApp.
entries.JackTheGit/hermes-autonomous-server: Targeting production environments on Ubuntu and Debian, this project configures the Hermes Gateway as a reboot-safe systemd service. It is optimized for headless operation and autonomous execution, enabling the agent to perform recurring AI cron jobs without manual intervention.
entries.ellickjohnson/portainer-stack-hermes: This deployment stack integrates Hermes with a ttyd web terminal, allowing users to interact with the agent's CLI directly through a browser. It is designed for easy management via Portainer and utilizes GitHub Actions for automated image builds and deployment.

entries.builderz-labs/mission-control: This self-hosted orchestration platform manages multi-agent workflows across frameworks like CrewAI and AutoGen with zero external dependencies. It is essential for Hermes users requiring local task dispatching, cost monitoring, and human-in-the-loop sign-offs via the Aegis review system.
entries.swarmclawai/swarmclaw: Swarmclaw enables the creation of autonomous agent swarms with native support for Hermes and over 20 other LLM providers. It distinguishes itself through durable agent memory and a visual organization chart that tracks how agents delegate tasks and learn new skills from conversations.
entries.howdymary/hermes-agent-metaharness: This specialized framework uses an automated outer-loop search process to optimize the harness code used for evaluating Hermes Agent. It orchestrates complex benchmark evaluations, such as TBLite, by mutating and comparing candidate code to find the most effective performance frontiers.
entries.runtimenoteslabs/gladiator: Gladiator creates a competitive arena where two autonomous AI companies, powered by Hermes Agent, compete for GitHub stars. The system orchestrates nine distinct agents and provides a real-time dashboard to visualize task execution, code generation, and live scoreboard metrics.
entries.1ilkhamov/opencode-hermes-multiagent: This project coordinates a pipeline of 17 specialized agents focused on research, infrastructure, and security. It ensures high-standard outputs by enforcing mandatory quality gates where reviewer and tester agents must approve work before it moves through the master orchestrator.
entries.supermodeltools/bigiron: Bigiron automates an eight-phase software development lifecycle by combining Hermes Agent with Supermodel MCP code graphs. It maintains architectural integrity by using graph-diff phase gates to validate structural changes at every step from planning to health checks.
entries.Rainhoole/hermes-agent-acp-skill: This framework implements ACP-style delegation to route tasks between Hermes subagents, Codex, and Claude Code. It focuses on operational safety by isolating delegation workflows to prevent context pollution and enforcing strict timeouts and output limits.

entries.junhoyeo/tokscale: This Rust-based CLI tool provides a high-performance TUI for tracking token consumption and costs across multiple AI agents, including Hermes and Claude Code. It features unique 3D contribution graph visualizations to help developers monitor their usage patterns across various model providers.
entries.joeynyc/hermes-skins: This utility allows developers to personalize the Hermes terminal interface through YAML-based configuration files for ASCII banners, spinners, and color schemes. It simplifies UI customization by allowing new themes to inherit from default settings while only defining specific visual overrides.
entries.AlexAI-MCP/hermes-CCC: This project ports the Hermes Agent brain and 46 core skills directly into the Claude Code Channel for native execution without external processes or OAuth requirements. It specifically targets MLOps workflows by including built-in tools for vLLM inference, GGUF management, and reinforcement learning training.
entries.unmodeled-tyler/vessel-browser: Designed for autonomous agent control via MCP, this AI-native browser provides a visible interface for human supervision and action rollback. It enhances agent reliability by inferring structured schemas for web elements and maintaining durable, named sessions for complex tasks.
entries.Abruptive/Ankh.md: Ankh.md introduces a scoped framework for Hermes, allowing developers to define project-specific memories, tools, and instructions within individual folders. It enables seamless switching between global settings and local configurations, ensuring agents remain context-aware within specific workspaces.
entries.roli-lpci/lintlang: This static linter ensures the structural integrity of agent configurations and system prompts by detecting seven distinct failure modes without making LLM calls. It provides deterministic validation for YAML and JSON files, making it an essential tool for cost-free CI/CD integration and HERM v1.1 scoring.
entries.0xNyk/openclaw-to-hermes: This migration utility streamlines the transition from OpenClaw to Hermes Agent by mapping complex model configurations and workspace files to the Hermes YAML format. It automates the transfer of agent personas, memories, and local credentials to ensure continuity across platforms.
entries.42-evey/evey-setup: This deployment script automates the creation of a full Hermes Agent stack using Docker, LiteLLM, and Ollama for local GPU inference. It comes pre-configured with 29 community plugins for memory and social integration, offering a zero-cost entry point for running agents with free model routing.

entries.nesquena/hermes-webui: This lightweight Python and vanilla JavaScript interface provides full feature parity with the Hermes CLI in a mobile-responsive web format. It features a three-panel layout that allows users to browse workspace files and view inline previews alongside their active agent sessions.
entries.EKKOLearnAI/hermes-web-ui: A comprehensive dashboard focused on multi-platform connectivity, offering unified configuration for eight channels including Telegram, Discord, and WhatsApp. It includes real-time session persistence, tool call expansion, and detailed analytics for tracking token usage and costs across different models.
entries.outsourc-e/hermes-workspace: Designed for zero-fork compatibility with vanilla Hermes installations, this workspace integrates a memory browser, terminal, and skills manager into a single interface. Its standout Swarm Mode enables role-based dispatching, allowing users to coordinate multiple persistent agents simultaneously.
entries.xaspx/hermes-control-interface: This self-hosted gateway acts as a command center for starting, stopping, and configuring individual agent profiles. It provides deep system visibility through an integrated xterm.js terminal, real-time system metrics, and granular token analytics for cost management.
entries.dodo-reach/hermes-desktop: A native macOS workspace that uses direct SSH connections to ensure the remote host remains the single source of truth for all agent data. The application organizes the Hermes experience through specialized views for Kanban boards, cron jobs, and profile-aware terminal sessions.
entries.clawvader-tech/hermes-telegram-miniapp: This mobile-first React dashboard is built specifically as a Telegram Mini App, offering a 10-page UI for managing agents on the go. It supports background spawning for up to five concurrent instances and includes visual gauges for monitoring CPU, memory, and disk usage.
entries.aivrar/portable-hermes-agent: A zero-install Windows GUI that operates without admin rights or Docker, focusing on local workflows like TTS, music generation, and ComfyUI. It features a dynamic tool maker for creating custom API wrappers and comes pre-loaded with over 100 specialized tools.
entries.fathah/hermes-desktop: This desktop companion simplifies the initial setup of Hermes Agent with a guided installer for local dependencies and environment configuration. It provides a unified GUI to manage 16 messaging gateways and 14 toolsets, complemented by full-text search for session history.
entries.sanchomuzax/hermes-webui: A monitoring-focused dashboard that provides a real-time activity feed and cost tracking for active Hermes sessions. It utilizes FTS5 search for deep inspection of conversation histories and allows users to manage scheduled cron jobs and read-only configurations.
entries.Euraika-Labs/pan-ui: This chat-first workspace emphasizes extensibility through an integrated marketplace for discovering and installing new skills and MCP servers. It features SSE streaming for real-time responses and profile-scoped management of global memory and agent runtimes.
entries.pyrate-llama/hermes-ui: A glassmorphic React 18 application that pairs a lightweight Python proxy with a modern aesthetic for self-hosted AI management. The interface supports real-time log streaming and a live workspace file browser, making it easy to preview files and manage MCP tools.
